title: Annikin Starkiller

'''Annikin Starkiller''' was the son of [[Kane Starkiller]] and the [[Jedi-Bendu]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan Learner]] to [[Luke Skywalker (The Star Wars)|Luke Skywalker]], as well as the protector and later, lover to the [[Princess/Legends|Princess]]/[[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of his home planet of [[Aquilae (The Star Wars)|Aquilae]], [[Leia (The Star Wars)|Leia]]. He was the hero of the battle which saw the defeat of the [[Galactic Empire (The Star Wars)|Galactic Empire]] in the [[Aquilaean system]].

==Biography==
After the [[Showdown on Coruscant|Jedi Rebellion]], the teenage Annikin and his brother [[Deak Starkiller]] settled on the [[Fourth Moon of Utapau]] with their father, [[Kane Starkiller]]. Annikin, despite his relative youth, had taken part in the [[Kessilian civil wars]] and led a [[Hubble expedition]] to the [[Cone systems]]. He wore his hair in a distinctive [[Kessilian hair knot]].

After a [[Knights of Sith|Sith Knight]] located him and his family and killed his brother, Deak, Annikin and Kane headed back home to [[Aquilae (The Star Wars)|Aquilae]].

He became the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] learner of Luke Skywalker after his father, Kane, revealed to Skywalker that he was dying.<ref name="TSW1" />

After the death of his father, he became the suitor to [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Leia (The Star Wars)|Leia]] of Aquilae, ruler of his home [[Planet/Legends|planet]], and was named [[Lord Protector of Aquilae]].

==Behind the scenes==
Annikin Starkiller was the lead character in the [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough draft]] of the script for what would become [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']]. He played a similar role to that of Luke Skywalker in ''A New Hope'', although his cockiness and brashness resembled [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from ''A New Hope'' and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] from [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']]. While there was nobody named Annikin in the [[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|second draft]], Annikin became the name of Luke's father in the [[The Star Wars: From The Adventures of Luke Starkiller|third draft]]<ref name="Making">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref> and Luke's father was identified as Anakin in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

In the [[The Star Wars first draft|first draft]], Annikin was renamed "'''Justin Valor'''." During the filming of ''A New Hope'', Luke's surname was changed from Starkiller to Skywalker, his Jedi byname in the second draft.<ref name="Making" />

It is unknown who Annikin's mother was or what happened to her, as she is implied to be dead.<ref name="Making" />
